claim_bullets.bullet_text: Given the title of this chapter, it is not a surprise that JavaScript makes this possible (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.230-231).
An iterator written in a generation style is called a generator (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.230-231).
It would be very nice if we could sometimes write iterators as a .next() method that gets called, and sometimes write out a generator (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.230-231).

source_record_text: This chapter covers JavaScript generators, including how to declare them using the function * syntax and how to use the yield keyword to produce values.
